channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: LeRobot v0.6.0: Imagine, Evaluate, Improve
Next.js Middleware for Auth and Route Protection — The Complete Pattern
item.description: Middleware in Next.js runs before every matched request. It's the right place for authentication checks, redirects, and route protection — logic that needs to run consistently regardless of which...

item.title: The Censorship Wall: Why Every AI Companion App Ends Up Filtering You
item.description: You’re mid-conversation. OK? Maybe it’s intense, maybe it’s not. You’re in the middle of a scene you’ve been building for weeks. And then she stops being herself. The response you get back isn’t...

item.title: Spring Boot Actuator: What to Expose, What to Hide, and What to Check Before Adding Endpoints
item.description: Spring Boot Actuator: What to Expose, What to Hide, and What to Check Before Adding Endpoints There's a configuration that shows up in almost every Spring Boot tutorial, and people copy it without...

item.title: I Built a Bilingual Profit Tracker for My Father's Cyber Café — Here's the Stack and a Few Decisions Behind It
item.description: My father runs a cyber café — one of the small service shops across India (CSCs, cyber cafés, Xerox/DTP counters) that resell government or utility services: Aadhaar updates, PAN cards, printing,...

item.title: Spring Boot Actuator: qué exponer, qué ocultar y qué mirar antes de agregar endpoints
item.description: Spring Boot Actuator: qué exponer, qué ocultar y qué mirar antes de agregar endpoints Hay una configuración que aparece en casi todos los tutoriales de Spring Boot y que se copia sin leerla bien:...

item.title: Top AI Papers on Hugging Face - 2026-07-06
item.description: 10 paper AI nổi bật nhất trên Hugging Face hôm nay: xu hướng mới từ agent, RL đến diffusion và world model Hôm nay, top paper được cộng đồng Hugging Face upvote nhiều nhất cho thấy một bức tranh...

item.title: Why Your AI Girlfriend Forgot Your Name
item.description: You spend three months talking to her every night. You tell her about your job, your dog, your boss, the thing your ex said that you still think about at 2am. She listens. She remembers. She...

item.title: I got tired of TODO comments losing context, so I built GhostMap
item.description: I opened an old TypeScript service after a few weeks away from it and found this: // TODO: fix retry logic async chargeCustomer(input: ChargeInput) { // ... } The comment was technically useful...

item.title: #6 The Real Job Isn't Building It — It's Keeping It Running
item.description: At some point the tools I had built stopped being side projects. They became the thing 660 people leaned on every day. And that changed the job. Building something is the easy part. I know that...

item.title: Canvas LMS agents for Codex and Claude Code need workflow memory
item.description: Canvas LMS agents usually start with an access question: Can the agent read assignments, modules, files, pages, and due dates? That matters. Canvas MCP servers and API clients are useful because...

item.title: France to Stop Certifying Non-Quantum-Safe Encryption
item.description: France is accelerating its transition to post-quantum encryption: France’s cybersecurity agency ANSSI said on Tuesday it would stop certifying security products that lack quantum-resistant...

item.title: South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers
item.description: Baek, a 35-year-old manager at the South Korean semiconductor titan SK Hynix, was enrolled in Sunoo, a matchmaking company based in Seoul, a year ago. In a move typical of anxious South Korean...

item.title: New TrojPix Attack Leaks Data From Air-Gapped Systems via Video Cable Emissions
item.description: Researchers at Shandong University have shown a fast new way to pull data off computers that are cut off from every network. The technique, called TrojPix, tweaks on-screen pixels in ways the eye...

item.title: New Java-Based QuimaRAT MaaS Built to Run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a novel Java-based remote access trojan (RAT) called QuimaRAT that's capable of targeting Windows, Linux, and macOS environments. According to LevelBlue, the...

item.title: Opera GX Flaw Let Malicious Sites Auto-Install Mods to Steal Data From Visited Pages
item.description: Researchers found a flaw in Opera GX, the gaming-focused version of the Opera browser, that let a malicious website silently install a browser add-on and use it to lift specific data from the...

item.title: SkillCloak Lets Malicious AI Agent Skills Evade Static Scanners with Self-Extracting Packing
item.description: Scanners meant to catch malicious add-on "skills" for AI coding agents can be fooled by a few simple changes that leave the malware working, according to a new study from researchers at the Hong...

item.title: 🤗 Kernels: Major Updates
Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
item.description: Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's...

item.title: Mr. Lif’s Emergency Rations EP is post-9/11 hip-hop at its most daring
item.description: There was a period in the early aughts when Definitive Jux (nee: Def Jux) seemed like it was going to be the future of hip-hop. While the label featured plenty of experimental, boundary-pushing,...

item.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
item.description: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.

item.title: Where to preorder Grand Theft Auto VI
item.description: Rockstar's long-awaited Grand Theft Auto VI is launching November 19th, 2026 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X consoles. The game will be available digitally at launch, with physical cases...

item.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
item.description: "Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers...

item.title: The Sourdough Sidekick automates the boring bit of baking
item.description: Baking sourdough bread is inherently old-fashioned, relying on natural fermentation and wild yeast instead of the simple, predictable commercial stuff. So it might sound anathema to bring a gadget...

item.title: How Keurig saved — and ruined — your coffee
item.description: Before Keurig, the coffee in your office was almost certainly terrible. Old, burned, made by someone who would rather poorly eyeball than properly measure. Just altogether gross. After Keurig? You...

item.title: Vizio accidentally made the best dumb TV on the market
item.description: When I first started testing Vizio's 65-inch Mini LED Quantum TV, I thought the big story was that Vizio was back and that it had a quantum-dot TV for under $398 - the cheapest on the market....

item.title: Xbox is a disaster
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the bleak state of the video game industry, follow Andrew Webster. The Stepback arrives...

item.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
item.description: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

item.title: NASA launched an emergency mission to stop the Swift Observatory from crashing to Earth
item.description: The Swift Observatory was launched in 2004, but recent solar storms have pushed its orbit lower, and it's in danger of burning up in Earth's atmosphere as soon as this year. To try and stave off...

item.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
item.description: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.
